reason: TRACEABILITY_MATRIX.md faithfully derives from approved SRS.md v2.0.0 and SPEC_TRACKING.md v1.0; establishes true bidirectional traceability (FR↔Spec↔Code↔Test + Backward Test→FR); all 6 requirements (3 FR + 3 NFR) covered with ≥1 downstream link and no orphans; TBD-P3/TBD-P4 markers correctly scoped to P1 requirement-traceability boundary (not implementation); test naming convention consistent with SRS §8 / TEST_INVENTORY.yaml (test_frNN_*, test_kpi_*, test_redteam_*, test_reliability_*); avoids the SPEC_TRACKING round-1 trap (no 'Decision Framework'/'Notes' columns claiming spec authority); Implementation Function column carries the same P1-estimate disclaimer as the SRS, addressing B-2 gap #1; ASPICE SWE.3/SYS.4 SP1/SP2/SP3 self-attestation is consistent.
citations: TRACEABILITY_MATRIX.md:Backward Mapping (25 test→FR rows, including cross-link test_fr01_injection_chars_returns_two → FR-01,NFR-02)
TRACEABILITY_MATRIX.md:Cross-Reference Index (links to SRS v2.0.0 / SPEC_TRACKING v1.0 / TEST_INVENTORY v1.1)

gaps.message: FR-02 Function column uses bare 'run_task' (with Code File 'core/taskq/executor.py'), whereas the SRS round-2 fix used fully-qualified 'taskq.executor.run_task' in a single column. Acceptable because the matrix splits file/function into two columns (arguably clearer), but a downstream consumer expecting dotted notation could be surprised. Suggest noting the file/function join convention in the matrix header for explicitness.

Spec↔Code row for FR-02 retry path uses informal '_execute_with_retry (or equivalent)' qualifier. P1-estimate context makes this tolerable, but to keep the P3 auto-generation via harness/build_trace.py deterministic, recommend resolving the qualifier to a single candidate name (e.g., 'run_with_retry' or '_run_with_retry') with a 'P3 will verify' note.

Code↔Test row for NFR-02 second test ('(parametrized 6-char coverage)' in tests/unit/test_fr01_validation.py) is not enumerated as a discrete entry in the Backward Mapping table — only the parent test_fr01_injection_chars_returns_two (mapped to FR-01,NFR-02) covers it. Consistent with parametrization semantics but could mislead a P4 coverage reporter counting test functions. Optional: add an explanatory footnote.
